description_long: This object is a blocky, stylized airplane with a predominantly white fuselage and a blue tail fin, reminiscent of a voxel or pixel-art design. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the midsection, with brown elements suggesting propeller engines mounted on each wing. The nose is squared with a small black detail that may represent a cockpit window or sensor. The tail features a vertical stabilizer with a stepped, pixelated pattern in blue and white, and a horizontal stabilizer at the base. The undercarriage includes small, dark elements that likely represent fixed landing gear.
description: A voxel-style white airplane with blue tail, brown propeller engines, and straight wings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the white fuselage, blue tail, brown propeller engines, and squared nose.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the straight wingspan, twin brown propeller engines, and narrow fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the blue tail, white body, and brown propeller engines.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the blue vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizer, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings with visible propellers.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers at the base.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with visible main and nose gear units.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit at the front of the fuselage with a single window feature.
